title: Daily Market Digest: May 7, 2026
subtitle: Wall Street edges higher on Mideast peace hopes and strong earnings; Tech leads gains.

sections.title: Market Pulse: US Stocks Edge Higher on Geopolitical Optimism

items.headline: Wall Street extends gains as US-Iran peace deal hopes rise and robust earnings buoy sentiment.
items.summary:
US stock markets hovered near record highs today, driven by increasing optimism for a potential agreement between the United States and Iran, which sent oil prices tumbling. Strong quarterly earnings from major companies also provided significant support. While major indices showed modest gains throughout the day, the underlying sentiment remained positive.

In contrast, Indian equity markets traded marginally lower amid continued foreign fund outflows and lingering geopolitical uncertainties.

Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Up 0.17% on hopes for US-Iran peace deal; hit fresh all-time intraday high.
Gained on the day, setting a fresh all-time intraday high, largely driven by tech strength.
Experienced slight fluctuations, rising in early trade but slipping midday, as value names lagged tech.

sections.title: Top Movers: Notable Gainers and Losers

items.headline: Datadog Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Datadog ($DDOG) surged 33.6% after the company reported better-than-expected quarterly profit estimates.

items.headline: Fluence Energy Jumps on Narrower Loss
items.summary: Fluence Energy ($FLNC) jumped 31% after reporting a narrower-than-estimated second-quarter adjusted Ebitda loss and maintaining its 2026 revenue forecast.

items.headline: Whirlpool Plunges on Weak Results
items.summary: Whirlpool ($WHR) plunged 14.5% to 18% after reporting weak quarterly results, cutting its full-year revenue forecast, and announcing a dividend cut. The appliance maker plans to raise prices and accelerate cost-cutting measures.

items.headline: Arm Holdings Tumbles on Smartphone Concerns
items.summary: Arm Holdings ($ARM) dropped 8% to 10% despite beating earnings estimates, as investors focused on weak fourth-quarter royalty revenue, hurt by sluggishness in the smartphone industry and broader demand concerns.

items.headline: Census Bureau Releases New Business Trends and Outlook Data; Jobless Claims Rise
items.summary: The U.S. Census Bureau today released new data from its Business Trends and Outlook Survey (BTOS), including insights into business adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Separately, US jobless claims rose last week, though less sharply than economists expected, while worker productivity grew at only half the anticipated pace.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Initiates Roundtable to Combat Payment Fraud
items.summary: The Federal Reserve, in collaboration with the Federal Communications Commission and the U.S. Treasury Department, is forming a public-private roundtable to solicit feedback on combating payments fraud. This initiative comes as the Fed sees a rising threat of payment fraud.

items.headline: Healthcare and Communication Services Lead Gains; Energy Lags
items.summary: In sector performance, Healthcare and Communication Services sectors demonstrated strength today, contributing to broader market gains. Conversely, the Energy sector lagged, with crude oil prices continuing to fall amidst geopolitical developments. Basic materials also experienced declines.

items.headline: Major Companies Report Quarterly Earnings
items.summary: Several companies reported Q1 2026 earnings today. Liberty Media ($FWONA, $FWONK) announced significant revenue increases for both F1 (+53%) and MotoGP (+25%). Evergy ($EVRG) reported strong Q1 results and reaffirmed its 2026 guidance. Patria Investments ($PAX) saw Fee Related Earnings jump 19%, while Xometry ($XMTR) posted record Q1 revenue growth of 36%.
